---
title: "Teaching/Learning"
---

I teach on intelligence, technology, data science, and international security — at
UC San Diego, and previously for policymakers and military audiences.

## Courses — UC San Diego

- *Spies: Intelligence and U.S. National Security* (undergraduate)
- *Theories of Technology and National Security* (undergraduate)
- *Data Science for National Security* (undergraduate; cross-listed with the Data Science program)
- *Field Seminar in International Security* (Ph.D. seminar)

## Courses — Executive & Policymaker

- *Military Applications of Data Science* — Department of Defense (2018)
- *Interstate Conflict* — U.S. Army contract; officers & government personnel (2015–2016)
- *Asia–Pacific Security* — U.S. Army contract (2015–2016)
- *Decision Making in Crisis* — U.S. Army contract (2014–2016)

## Advice for Students

Short, practical posts on research, writing, methods, and navigating the field —
a running series I'll add to over time. *Coming soon.*
